channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: India’s 25,000 Crore MALE UAV Program Nears RFQ Stage as Bids Arrive Amid Global Disruptions
item.description: India’s ambitious tri-service requirement for 87 MALE UAVs is entering a decisive phase, with the Ministry of Defence preparing to transition from the Request for Proposal (RFP) stage to the Request for Quote (RFQ) by mid-2026. The RFP, issued in November 2025, invited detailed bids from Indian companies—most of whom have partnered with established foreign OEMs such as Elbit Systems, Israel Aerospace Industries (IAI), and General Atomics.
item.dc:creator: RAUNAK KUNDE

item.title: Akash-1S Enters Malaysia’s Air Defence Race as India Pushes Indigenous SAM with Seeker Advantage
item.description: India has formally pitched its Akash surface-to-air missile system to Malaysia, signaling a renewed push to expand defence exports into Southeast Asia. According to information available to idrw.org, Bharat Dynamics Limited (BDL) and Bharat Electronics Limited (BEL) showcased both the Akash-1S and Akash Prime variants during the Defence Services Asia (DSA) 2026 exhibition held from April 20–23, targeting Malaysia’s emerging requirement for a medium-range air defence system.
item.dc:creator: RAUNAK KUNDE

item.title: Flying Wedge Unveils Plans for AI-Piloted Jet UCAV with ‘Mobbing’ Swarm Tactics, Backed by Tejas Engineers
item.description: Indian startup Flying Wedge Defence and Aerospace has claimed that it is developing a high subsonic, jet-powered unmanned combat aerial vehicle (UCAV) designed around artificial intelligence-driven operations and advanced swarm warfare concepts. According to the company, the platform will be approximately 6 meters in length, making it significantly smaller than the Tejas light combat aircraft, and is being positioned as a next-generation autonomous combat system.
item.dc:creator: RAUNAK KUNDE

item.title: HAL Issues Tender for LCA Mk2 Prototype 2 Components, Signals Acceleration of Indigenous Fighter Program
item.description: Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) has initiated a fresh industrial outreach for the LCA Tejas Mk2 program, issuing a tender for CNC machining and supply of components specifically for the second prototype (PV2). The document underscores a clear push toward distributed manufacturing and tighter timelines as India accelerates development of its next-generation indigenous fighter.
item.dc:creator: AFI

item.title: DRDO Chief Confirms ‘Sudarshan Chakra’ Feasibility Report Ready; Project Moves to Defence Minister for Approval
item.description: India’s push toward next-generation strategic defence capabilities has taken a significant step forward, with DRDO Chairman Dr. Samir V Kamat confirming that the feasibility report for the ambitious ‘Sudarshan Chakra’ programme has been completed and is now set to be reviewed by the Raksha Mantri. The development marks a crucial transition from conceptual groundwork to potential programmatic approval, placing the project on the cusp of formal execution within India’s evolving defence architecture.
item.dc:creator: AFI

item.title: Pakistan’s Cyber Posture in the Nuclear Shadow: An Asymmetric Strategy Built on Ambiguity, Influence, and Controlled Escalation
item.description: In the nuclearised environment of South Asia, cyberspace has become Pakistan’s most agile and preferred domain of competition. Here, deniability, narrative shaping, and sub-threshold operations often deliver greater strategic returns than direct kinetic firepower. Pakistan’s approach to cyberwarfare is neither random nor immature; it forms a deliberate, intelligence-led extension of its long-established asymmetric and hybrid warfare doctrine. Despite the absence of a publicly declared formal cyber doctrine, Islamabad has developed a flexible model that emphasises low-cost, high-impact actions calibrated to avoid crossing red lines while maintaining relevance against a conventionally and technologically superior adversary.
item.dc:creator: AFI

item.title: Tata LPTA 3945 8×8: India’s Next-Generation High Mobility Platform Powering Multi-Domain Operations
item.description: In the demanding landscapes of modern warfare—from scorching deserts and rugged mountains to contested forward areas—the Tata LPTA 3945 8×8 stands as a testament to indigenous engineering excellence. Developed by Tata Advanced Systems, this rugged high-mobility vehicle is engineered to deliver unmatched performance in the toughest missions, serving as a versatile backbone for specialized military applications across the Indian Armed Forces.
item.dc:creator: AFI

item.title: Shield AI announces opening of an office in New Delhi
item.description: Shield AI announces opening of an office in New Delhi to support its growing partnership with the Indian Ministry of Defence and advance its work with the broader industry ecosystem. Shield AI has established a wholly owned subsidiary, Shield AI India, to support software integration, engineering, and autonomy development, and to grow indigenous engineering and software development capabilities for India.
item.dc:creator: Company Press Release

item.title: Bharat Forge Limited to acquire 30% stake in Fortuna Engineering Private Limited under a strategic partnership
item.description: Established in 1989, Fortuna Engineering Private Limited is a leading manufacturer of precision-machinedengine components for automotive and industrial applications in India. The company supplies to majorautomotive OEMs in India across the passenger vehicle, commercial vehicle, tractor and off-highwaysegments.
item.dc:creator: Company Press Release

item.title: Pakistan navy to add 8 advanced Chinese submarines to boost maritime security
item.description: The subs are in addition to Chinese-made J10-C fighter jets, which were war-tested for the first time during last year’s Pakistan-India conflict.
item.dc:creator: REUTERS

item.title: Joint India-US Venture NISAR Shows Mexico City Sinking At Alarming Pace
item.description: The findings, based on early observations from the NISAR (NASA-ISRO Synthetic Aperture Radar) satellite, underscore both the severity of the capital’s long-running subsidence crisis and the growing capability of space-based monitoring systems to track ground movement in near real time, a NASA media release said Wednesday (local time).
item.dc:creator: IANS

item.title: Defence Veterans Support Great Nicobar Project, Call It Vital For India’s Strategic, Economic Interests
item.description: LoP Gandhi flagged potential ecological damage and risks to indigenous communities in the islands. However, former Air Chief Marshal R. K. S. Bhadauria rejected the allegations, describing them as “baseless” and lacking a comprehensive understanding of the project’s scope and long-term significance.
item.dc:creator: IANS

item.title: As Terror Networks Converge Across West Asia, India Emerges As A Trusted Balancer
item.description: In an interaction titled “Crisis in the Middle East: Are We Entering a New Regional Order?”, Egyptian parliamentarian Mohamed M Farid told think-tank Usanas Foundation CEO, Dr Abhinav Pandya, that India’s role as a constructive partner will be welcomed in the region.
item.dc:creator: IANS

item.title: ‘JeM Hit Again’: Top Commander Salman Dead As Mysterious Killings Mount
item.description: Some of the major attacks that he was involved in included the 2001 Parliament attack and the 2019 Pulwama attack that targeted personnel of the CRPF.
item.dc:creator: IANS

item.title: ‘Enough is enough’: Farooq Abdullah tells Pakistan to end cycle of violence in J-K
item.description: “My humble advice to both these nations, my own nation and Pakistan, is that the time has come. Let us leave the path of violence and start finding ways of peace so that we can live with honour and dignity,” the 88-year-old told PTI in an interview.
item.dc:creator: PTI
